title: 仓颉造字
description: The legend of how Chinese writing was born from nature.
ending: And so the world was written into being — one stroke at a time.
story_text: 上古时代，黄帝的史官仓颉，生来有四只眼睛。
他仰观天上的日月星辰，
看见月光洒在大地之上。
他从万物的形状中，创造了文字。
天地为之震动，鬼神为之哭泣。
从此，人类有了记录世界的方式。

layers.description: 日 — sun. Cang Jie looked up and saw the sun arc across the sky. He drew its circle, and the character for sun was born.
layers.narration_line: 他仰观天上的日月星辰

layers.description: 山 — mountain. Three peaks reached toward heaven. He saw their silhouette and drew three strokes rising from the earth.
水 — water. The river wound between stones, splitting and rejoining. Its flowing lines became the character for water.
木 — tree. A lone tree stood at the river's edge — trunk, branches reaching, roots holding. One stroke, then two.
人 — person. A figure walked between the trees, two legs carrying them forward. The simplest character — two strokes.
月 — moon. As night fell, the moon rose and cast silver across the world Cang Jie had been watching all day.
layers.narration_line: 看见月光洒在大地之上

_narration_guide.note: Record or synthesise narration in Mandarin. Suggested text per layer:
lines: 仓颉仰望天空，看见太阳。他画了一个圆，日字诞生了。
他俯视大地，看见三座山峰。山字，就是三峰的形状。
他听见水声，看见河流蜿蜒。水字，流动而生。
林中一棵大树，枝干分明。木字，根与枝的写照。
树下走过一个人，两腿迈步。人字，最简单，也最深刻。
夜幕降临，月亮升起。月字，如月的缺口。
